title: Sourdough Bread Data: Average Hydration, Temperature &
description: Original research analyzing 85 sourdough recipes. Average hydration, flour types, fermentation times, and regional baking traditions compared.

mainEntity.name: What is the average hydration for sourdough bread?

acceptedAnswer.text: Based on analysis of 85 sourdough recipes, the average hydration is 77.8%, with a median of 77.0%. Most recipes fall in the 70-85% range.

mainEntity.name: What is the best flour for sourdough bread?

acceptedAnswer.text: Bread flour is the most commonly used, appearing in 38% of recipes. All-purpose flour is second at 25%, followed by whole wheat blends at 14%.

mainEntity.name: What temperature should I bake sourdough bread?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most common baking temperature is 450°F (232°C), used by 53% of recipes. The average across all recipes is 431°F.

mainEntity.name: Should I use a Dutch oven for sourdough?

acceptedAnswer.text: A significant portion of sourdough recipes call for a Dutch oven. It traps steam for better oven spring, crispier crust, and more consistent results.

mainEntity.name: How long should sourdough bulk ferment?

acceptedAnswer.text: Bulk fermentation typically ranges from 3-5 hours at room temperature (72-78°F). Cold retard extends this to 12-18 hours for more complex flavor.

schemas.headline: Sourdough Bread Data: Average Hydration, Temperature & Flour Types (85 Recipes Analyzed)
schemas.description: Original research analyzing 85 sourdough recipes for hydration, flour types, oven temperatures, and baking methods
